mechanism: SL binds D14 alpha/beta hydrolase, induces conformational change and formation of D14-D3-D53 complex
D14 recruits SCF-D3 E3 ligase to ubiquitinate D53 for 26S proteasomal degradation
D3 is the F-box subunit of SCF complex that ubiquitinates D53; required for proteasomal degradation
D53 binds IPA1 via direct protein-protein interaction and represses IPA1 transcriptional activation activity
D53 physically interacts with MOC1 to repress tiller bud outgrowth
D53 represses OsCKX9 expression; SL-induced D53 degradation derepresses OsCKX9
IPA1 directly binds GTAC motif in OsTB1 promoter via SBP domain and activates transcription
IPA1 directly binds GTAC element in D53 promoter and activates D53 transcription (negative feedback loop with D53 protein repressing IPA1)
IPA1 directly binds OsPIN1b promoter to activate auxin efflux carrier expression
OsmiR156 cleaves IPA1/OsSPL14 mRNA via miR156 recognition site in 3' region

IPI1 stabilises OsSPL14/IPA1 protein in shoot apex via K63-linked polyubiquitination (tissue-specific: degrades in panicle but stabilises in shoot); ipi1-CRISPR -> reduced IPA1 accumulation -> increased tillering
OsSHI1 physically interacts with IPA1 and reduces IPA1 DNA binding affinity, inhibiting transcriptional activation activity
D27 is iron-containing beta-carotene isomerase, first step of SL biosynthesis converting all-trans to 9-cis-beta-carotene
D10 encodes CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) cleaving 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal to carlactone
D17/HTD1 encodes CCD7 catalyzing cleavage of 9-cis-beta-carotene to 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal
Rice MAX1 homologs (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100) convert carlactone to 4-deoxyorobanchol
OsTB1/FC1 is a TCP family transcription factor expressed in axillary buds that inhibits bud outgrowth; key branching integrator downstream of SL
OsMADS57 binds CArG motif in D14 promoter and directly suppresses D14 transcription
miR444a cleaves OsMADS57 mRNA
TAD1 is Cdh1-type APC/C co-activator; recruits MOC1 to APC/C E3 ligase for cell-cycle-dependent ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation
TE is CDC20-type APC/C co-activator; mediates ubiquitin-26S proteasome degradation of MOC1
SLR1 DELLA protein physically binds MOC1 and protects it from APC/C-mediated degradation
SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 binding, preventing NGR5 ubiquitination and degradation
NGR5 recruits PRC2 to OsTB1 promoter depositing H3K27me3 repressive mark
NGR5-PRC2 complex represses D14 via H3K27me3
Nitrogen supply (nitrate sensing via NRT1.1B) promotes NGR5 accumulation and PRC2 recruitment
OsTCP19 TCP TF directly binds DLT promoter and represses its transcription; inhibits N-responsive tillering
DLT is GRAS-family BR signaling TF promoting tiller outgrowth
N-induced OsLBD37 represses OsTCP19 transcription, relieving DLT repression and enabling N-induced tillering
High nitrogen induces OsLBD37 transcription
OsCKX2/Gn1a irreversibly degrades cytokinin (N6-isopentenyladenine, trans-zeatin)
OsCKX9 degrades cytokinin; nuclear/cytosolic dual-localized enzyme

CK represses TB1 via protein-level destabilisation independent of the HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1 phosphorelay. Kept as direct edge because the canonical signalling cascade carries the opposite (positive) sign through IPA1->TB1(+1) and so cannot account for CK's tillering-promoting (TB1-repressing) effect. Consequence: hk4+trans-zeatin rescue tests will show spurious direct-arm rescue (TRAP 5 framework limitation).
OsIPT genes (especially OsIPT4, OsIPT7) catalyze rate-limiting step of cytokinin biosynthesis (adenylate isopentenyltransferase)
OsLOG converts inactive cytokinin nucleotides to active free-base form
CK binds OsHK4 histidine kinase CHASE domain activating phosphorelay
Activated OsHK4 phosphorylates OsAHP1/2 histidine phosphotransfer proteins
OsAHP phosphorelay activates type-B OsRR21 response regulator for target gene transcription
Type-B OsRR21 directly binds CK response elements in IPA1 promoter activating transcription
OsPIN1b auxin efflux transporter, knockout shows increased tillers
OsPIN9 ammonium-induced auxin efflux transporter promotes tiller bud outgrowth
Ammonium specifically induces OsPIN9 expression (not nitrate)
OsCCA1 circadian MYB-like TF binds CBS element in OsTB1 promoter
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter
OsCCA1 binds CBS in D10 promoter
OsCCA1 binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter
OsPRR1 pseudo-response regulator represses OsCCA1 in reciprocal feedback loop
OsCCA1 binds CBS in OsPRR1 promoter and represses expression (part of evening loop)
Sucrose signaling stabilizes D53 protein (antagonizes SL-induced degradation)
Photosynthetic sugars downregulate OsCCA1 in roots and buds (tissue-dependent)
MOC2 encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 for sucrose biosynthesis pathway
LAX1 bHLH TF required for axillary meristem initiation; non-cell-autonomous movement essential
FON2 CLV3-like peptide restricts TAB1 expression domain via CLAVATA-like receptor signaling
MOC3 (TAB1/OsWUS) physically interacts with MOC1 to activate FON1
FON1 (CLAVATA1 ortholog) specifically affects tiller bud elongation; fon1 forms buds but fails to elongate

OsBZR1 (BR-activated TF, stabilised by BZR1-D53 complex at OsTB1 promoter) net-activates OsTB1 transcription in axillary meristem. The tiller-repression phenotype upon BR treatment requires a positive BZR1->TB1 edge given the canonical TB1->Tiller_Number repression.
BR stabilizes OsBZR1 via inhibition of OsBIN2 kinase (dephosphorylated BZR1)
ABA promotes bud dormancy
DST zinc finger protein binds DBS motif in OsCKX2 promoter and activates transcription; reg1 allele loses activation but retains binding
P-sufficient conditions repress SL biosynthesis genes D27, D10, D17; P-deficiency elevates them
P deficiency elevates D10/CCD8 expression
P deficiency elevates D17 expression
Decapitation removes apical auxin source, releasing axillary buds from apical dominance
N sufficiency represses SL biosynthesis genes D10, D17, D27
N sufficiency represses D17/HTD1 SL biosynthesis
N sufficiency represses D27 SL biosynthesis
Light signal entrains OsCCA1 expression (canonical circadian clock input)
Photoperiod regulates OsPRR1 evening expression
OsTAR2 is TAA/TAR aminotransferase; first step of IAA biosynthesis from tryptophan to IPA
OsYUCCA enzymes catalyze rate-limiting step of IAA biosynthesis (IPA to IAA)
MOC1 positively affects axillary meristem gene network including LAX1
Sucrose synthesis generates trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P) signaling molecule
T6P signaling represses BRC1/OsTB1 expression
NRT1.1B-dependent phosphorylation promotes NGR5 stability (nitrate-sensing-to-chromatin)
OsGA20ox2 (SD1) catalyzes multiple oxidation steps in GA biosynthesis
Type-A OsRRs provide negative feedback on CK signaling
BR signaling activates DLT expression/function in tiller promotion
NGR5 binds IPA1/OsSPL14 promoter and recruits PRC2 complex to deposit H3K27me3 mark; ChIP-qPCR direct binding confirmed; nitrogen dose-dependent
Photosynthesis-derived sucrose activates NGR5 expression at the shoot base, providing carbon-nitrogen integration for tiller bud elongation
DTN1 (Decreased Tiller Number 1) encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase and is required for photosynthesis and sucrose accumulation in shoot base / axillary buds
OsVIL2 chromatin-interacting factor binds OsTB1 promoter and represses OsTB1 expression via chromatin modification, releasing axillary bud outgrowth
OsGATA8 transcription factor represses OsTCP19 (negative regulator of tillering), promoting productive tiller formation under N-replete conditions
Os1900 (one of five rice MAX1-like paralogs) catalyzes carlactone-to-carlactonoic acid conversion together with Os5100; Os1900 is the N-fertilization-responsive paralog
Fertilization (N supply) transcriptionally upregulates Os1900 promoter; promoter mutations that disrupt N-induction increase tiller numbers under low fertilizer
MOC1 physically interacts with MOC3 (OsWUS/TAB1) and acts as a transcriptional co-activator; MOC1 cannot bind FON1 promoter alone but cooperates with MOC3 binding
T20 encodes Î¶-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO) acting upstream of D27 in the carotenoid pathway; required for SL precursor supply
GA binds GID1 nuclear receptor; GA-GID1 forms the active complex that subsequently recruits SCF-GID2 to ubiquitinate SLR1 and NGR5.
GA-GID1 complex promotes SLR1 (rice DELLA) interaction with F-box SCF-GID2 for polyubiquitination and 26S proteasomal degradation.
GA-GID1 complex directly targets NGR5 (Nitrogen-mediated tiller Growth Response 5) for 26S proteasomal degradation, coupling GA signalling to N-responsive tiller-promoting transcription.

Auxin (IAA) binds the TIR1/OsAFB F-box receptor in the SCF-TIR1 complex; IAA-TIR1 then ubiquitinates OsIAA repressors for proteasomal degradation. Represented as activating edge onto TIR1 to gate all auxin transcriptional effects.

Summary edge: active TIR1 derepresses OsIAA-OsARF axis; derepressed OsARFs bind D10 (CCD8) promoter to promote SL biosynthesis gene transcription. Collapsed through TIR1 to avoid proliferating OsIAA/OsARF intermediates that the curated set does not fully specify.
Summary edge: active TIR1 derepresses OsIAA-OsARF; OsARFs activate D17 (CCD7, HTD1) transcription. Auxin's SL-biosynthesis upregulation routed through TIR1 to preserve Perception Gate purity.

Summary edge: active TIR1 degrades OsIAA, releasing OsARFs; auxin-responsive OsARFs repress IPT (CK biosynthesis) transcription. Classic apical-auxin-represses-CK-biosynthesis mechanism routed through TIR1.

Canonical two-component phosphorelay: AHP (histidine phosphotransfer) phosphorylates type-A RRs, which accumulate as primary CK-response transcriptional targets. Replaces direct Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA bypass.
IPA1 directly binds OsLOG promoter and first intron to activate LOG (cytokinin-activating enzyme / lonely guy) transcription.
IPA1 activates OsHK4 via binding TGGGCC motif in its promoter with TCP co-factors PCF1/PCF2; forms IPA1-HK4 positive feedback arm of CK signalling.
HOX12 homeodomain-leucine-zipper TF directly induces OsNCED1 expression to drive ABA biosynthesis in shoot base; part of SL-induced parallel ABA-mediated tillering-repression arm.
OsNCED1 (9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase 1) catalyses the rate-limiting cleavage of 9-cis-xanthophyll to xanthoxin in ABA biosynthesis.

ABA represses SL biosynthesis genes including D10 (and D17, D27) via transcriptional downregulation; ABA biosynthetic mutants (aba1, aba2) show elevated SL biosynthesis, producing negative ABA-to-SL feedback.
OsHK4 CK receptor phosphorelay activates B-type response regulator OsRR21 which binds OsTB1 promoter and represses transcription in axillary meristem; ohk4 LOF de-represses OsTB1 -> reduced tillering
